t_ch: 对啊，你确实回答了他的疑问，当然有资格拿走这些，就连最为公正的塔兰顿都没法因此指责你。感谢雅努斯的馈赠吧。
t_en: That's right. You answered her questions, did you not? Of course, you have earned the right to take these. Not even the most just Talanton could blame you. Let us thank Janus for these gifts.

t_ch: （钻进木马的肚子里）
t_en: (Climb inside the wooden horse's stomach)

t_ch: 莫名地，你突然开始怀念起在木马肚子里度过的时光，安静、幽暗，带着些微的温暖，你意识到那就是你始终在追寻的家的感觉。
t_en: Suddenly, you are overcome with a mysterious nostalgia for the time that you spent inside the wooden horse's stomach. The quiet, the darkness, the subtle warmth... Subconsciously, you realize that it was the feeling of home that you had been searching for.

t_ch: 因此…你再次钻了进去。
t_en: So... you climb inside once more.

t_ch: 汹涌而来的困意包裹了你，就连受塞纳托斯赐福的永眠都不可能有如此地甜美，你…安安稳稳地在木马肚子里睡了个好觉。
t_en: A torrent of drowsiness washes over you. Not even an eternal sleep gifted by Thanatos could feel this sweet. You... drift into a deep and peaceful slumber inside the belly of the wooden horse.

t_ch: 这不是无名客该做的事！
t_en: This is not something a Nameless should be doing!

t_ch: 这次你总算说对了。别在这匹木马上耗费时间，还是去做点更「开拓」的事吧。
t_en: That's right for once. No more wasting time on this wooden horse. Time to go and do something more fitting of the Trailblaze.

t_ch: 你曾藏身其中的木马。供品已经被拿走，那位信徒也不见踪影，不会再有人来打扰你了。
t_en: The wooden horse that you were hidden inside. The offerings have been taken, and the disciple is nowhere to be seen. No one will come and disturb you again.

t_ch: （钻进木马的肚子里）
t_en: (Climb inside the wooden horse's stomach)

t_ch: 莫名地，你突然开始怀念起在木马肚子里度过的时光，安静、幽暗，带着些微的温暖，你意识到那就是你始终在追寻的家的感觉。
t_en: Suddenly, you are overcome with a mysterious nostalgia for the time that you spent inside the wooden horse's stomach. The quiet, the darkness, the subtle warmth... Subconsciously, you realize that it was the feeling of home that you had been searching for.

t_ch: 因此…你再次钻了进去。
t_en: So... you climb inside once more.

t_ch: 汹涌而来的困意包裹了你，就连受塞纳托斯赐福的永眠都不可能有如此地甜美，你…安安稳稳地在木马肚子里睡了个好觉。
t_en: A torrent of drowsiness washes over you. Not even an eternal sleep gifted by Thanatos could feel this sweet. You... drift into a deep and peaceful slumber inside the belly of the wooden horse.

t_ch: 这不是无名客该做的事！
t_en: This is not something a Nameless should be doing!

t_ch: 这次你总算说对了。别在这匹木马上耗费时间，还是去做点更「开拓」的事吧。
t_en: That's right for once. No more wasting time on this wooden horse. Time to go and do something more fitting of the Trailblaze.

t_ch: 毫无疑问，你曾在故事里见过这个庞大的木制道具，瞧它那高高昂起的头颅，以及宽广到足以装进好几个人的空洞腹部——所有特征都与你的认知相符。
t_en: There is no doubt about it. The giant wooden prop before you is one you recognize from an old story. Its head raised high in the sky and room in its stomach for several people — it has all the familiar characteristics you expect.

t_ch: 但当你尝试钻进木马的肚子里时，却发现…那地方的木板纹丝不动，无法从这一侧打开。
t_en: But, just as you try to climb inside the wooden horse's stomach, you find... The wooden board is stuck fast, and it can't be opened from this side.

t_ch: 用力踹！
t_en: Kick it hard!

t_ch: 砰砰——你深吸口气，转身便是狠狠几脚蹬在木马身上，可惜并未取得任何成效。恍神间，你似乎听到了轻微的呻吟声。
t_en: Bang! Bang! You take a deep breath, spin on your heel, and throw a number of ferocious kicks at the body of the horse. Unfortunately, to no avail. Suddenly, you hear a faint moan.

t_ch: （尝试把木马拖走）
t_en: (Try to drag the wooden horse)

t_ch: 嘎…吱…尽管你使出了全身力气，木马的车轮仍旧像是牢牢固定在地面上，并未产生任何位移。恍神间，你似乎听到了轻微的吸气声。
t_en: Creak... Creak... You try to pull with all your strength, but the wheels on the wooden horse remain firmly fixed in place. They don't seem to move at all. Suddenly, you hear a faint moan.

t_ch: 别、别…停下！我只是稍作歇息，很快就离开，不会造成任何影响，请放心。
t_en: No, don't... Stop! I'm just taking a quick break. I'll be gone in a moment! This will not affect anything. Please don't worry.

t_ch: 我是受{RUBY_B#门径之泰坦}雅努斯{RUBY_E#}赐福的神驹，能自由前往任何地方。滞留在这里，是因为遭到黑潮气息的污染，迷失了方向，不久便能恢复。
t_en: I am a divine foal that has received the blessing of {RUBY_B#Passage Titan}Janus{RUBY_E#}. I can travel freely between any lands. I am stranded here because I was infected by the black tide's essence and lost my sense of direction. I will recover soon.

t_ch: 我的灵智仍旧不太清晰，对位置的判断也颇为失准。请问——疯王的祭典在何处举办？我是否来到了错误的地点？
t_en: My mind is still unclear, and I have lost my sense of direction. May I ask... Where is the festival of the Mad King being held? Have I come to the wrong place?

t_ch: 你来晚了。
t_en: You're too late.

t_ch: 悬锋祭典早就结束了。
t_en: The festival of Kremnos ended a long time ago.

t_ch: 是么。难怪始终没遇见除你外的任何活物，原来…我在传送途中迷失了如此久的时间，以至于历史都发生了改变。这究竟……
t_en: I see. No wonder you are the only living thing I have come across this whole time. I see... I lost so much time when I was lost in teleportation, and even history itself has changed. Just what exactly...

t_ch: 尽管木马的嘴部没有活动，但你仿佛听到了一声怅然的叹息…从遥远的过去传来。
t_en: Though the wooden horse's mouth does not move, you feel as if you hear a mournful sigh... A sigh that echoes from the distant past.

t_ch: 我的使命不可能再完成，我的路也要走到尽头了。陌生人，愿未来的门扉始终向你敞开，不会像我这样，被命运隔绝在紧闭的门外。
t_en: I can never complete my mission, and my path has come to an end. Stranger, I wish that the door of tomorrow remains ever open to you. That you will not end up like me, isolated from fate on the other side of its door.

t_ch: 还储有最后一次传送的神力，就让我完整看看这座战争城邦的模样吧。这里是祭司们心心念念的地方，也是部族生存的最后希望…再见。
t_en: I still have enough power to teleport one final time, so allow me to see the complete state of this war-torn city. This was the land that the priests cherished, and the final hope for my tribe's survival... Farewell.

t_ch: 毫无生气的木马，看起来就是完全的死物。意外的是，你在它的外壳上发现了几行文字。
t_en: The lifeless wooden horse looks completely inanimate. To your surprise, you notice a few lines of writing on its outer body.

t_ch: 「我乃雅努萨波利斯的神驹，黑潮逼近之际，奉命前往悬锋城求援，为部族寻求共同抵抗祸乱的盟友。然而，灾厄气息扰乱了我的灵智，我在传送途中迷失了。」
t_en: "I am a divine foal of Janusopolis. As the black tide approached, I was ordered to go to Castrum Kremnos for aid and to find allies for my tribe to fight back against the impending disaster. However, the disastrous essence affected my senses and I became lost in teleportation."

t_ch: 「感官恢复时，我已身处破落的悬锋城，这里没有活物，只有失心的眷属在游荡。」
t_en: "When I regained my senses, I found myself in a deserted Castrum Kremnos. It lay in ruins and there was no life to be found. Only mindless Titankin wandered its ground."

t_ch: 「疯王已陨落，不会再有谁来拯救我的城邦了。部族肯定早就毁灭了吧，城里的人们呢，大祭司呢，其余神驹呢？我失败了。失败了。失败了。失败了……」
t_en: "The Mad King has fallen. No one will come to save my city now. I'm certain my tribe has long since been annihilated. What happened to its people? The High Priest? The other divine foals? I failed. Failed. Failed. Failed..."

t_ch: 毫无疑问，你曾在故事里见过这个庞大的木制道具，瞧它那高高昂起的头颅，以及宽广到足以装进好几个人的空洞腹部——所有特征都与你的认知相符。
t_en: No doubt you recognize this massive wooden prop from tales of old — its proud head held high, its cavernous belly large enough to hold several people — every detail matches the stories you know.

t_ch: 不过，木马的肚子里传来了窸窸窣窣的声响，似乎有人正躲藏在里面。
t_en: But wait — rustling sounds are coming from inside the wooden horse, suggesting someone might be hiding within.

t_ch: （敲敲木板）
t_en: (Knock on the wooden panels)

t_ch: 这不是无名客该做的事！
t_en: This is not something a Nameless should be doing!

t_ch: 确实，这匹平平无奇的木马并没能勾起你对它进行「开拓」的兴趣。但没关系，它似乎不会自己跑掉，等有空的时候再回来看看吧。
t_en: True, this plain wooden horse hasn't piqued your Trailblazing curiosity. No matter, though — it seems firmly planted here, so you can always return when time permits.

t_ch: …是负责整备的辅祭？免去那些繁文缛节吧，我只是回来稍作歇息，很快就离开。
t_en: ...Oh, you're the maintenance acolyte? No need for formalities — I'm just here for a brief rest before leaving.

t_ch: 说起来，我上月提出的改装诉求有后续进展吗？
t_en: Speaking of which, any updates on my modification proposal from last month?

t_ch: …什么改装？
t_en: ...What modification?

t_ch: 看来又被延后了，也罢，命运三相殿的行政速度实在配不上雅努斯的名号。
t_en: Delayed again, I see. The Temple of the Three Fates operates with a sluggishness unbefitting of Janus' name.

t_ch: 他们说不准随意改变外观。
t_en: They've forbidden any changes to the appearance.

t_ch: 我猜也是。呵，「门」的侍奉者却如此守旧，实在配不上雅努斯的名号。
t_en: Just as I thought. Hmph, how ironic that the servants of the "Passage" are so set in their ways — quite unworthy of Janus' name.

t_ch: 既然要踏上传递神谕之旅，自然得有气势。所以我提议将神驹整体涂为红色，不仅易于辨认，也适合传颂圣女的名号。
t_en: When carrying prophecies across the land, appearance matters. That's why I proposed painting the divine foal red — striking to behold and perfect for heralding the Maiden's name.

t_ch: 但大祭司认为外观改造并无必要，反而给神驹安上了车轮，实在令人费解。神驹能随意前往世界各处，怎会需要这种代步工具？可笑！
t_en: Yet the High Priest rejected the appearance changes and opted to add wheels instead — how perplexing! What use are wheels to a divine foal that can travel anywhere at will? Preposterous!

t_ch: 红色确实帅。
t_en: Yeah, red would've been pretty cool.

t_ch: 同意，还能给人一种迅捷的印象。
t_en: Right? It would really emphasize a sense of swiftness, too.

t_ch: 装轮子更重要一些吧？
t_en: But aren't wheels more essential?

t_ch: 车轮只是装饰，那些古板的祭司怎会明白？
t_en: The wheels are just for show — those stubborn priests wouldn't understand.

t_ch: 本还想与你细聊，但距离取得拉冬城的协助只差最后一步了。
t_en: I'd enjoy talking longer, but we're nearly there with gaining Ladon's support.

t_ch: 我的使命在呼唤我。回见，年轻的辅祭，愿未来的门扉始终向你敞开。
t_en: My calling beckons. Farewell, young acolyte — may the door of tomorrow remain ever open to you.

t_ch: 古老的言语还在耳畔回荡，但活窗已然合上。恍神间，你似乎看到木马的周身亮起微光，像是无形的门扉正在开启，难以用言语描述的空洞感忽地涌上心头。
t_en: The ancient words linger in the air, but the sliding window has been sealed shut. In your daze, you notice a faint glow around the wooden horse, as if an invisible portal opens — an ineffable emptiness suddenly grips your heart.

t_ch: 你意识到，方才与你对话的存在已经远去。但过段时间回来看看，或许…会有新的发现？
t_en: You realize the being that just spoke to you has disappeared. But maybe when you return after some time, there might be... new discoveries?

t_ch: 好难受…唔……
t_en: Nngh... I don't feel good...

t_ch: 你没事吧？
t_en: Are you okay?

t_ch: 我来帮你把门打开！
t_en: I can help you with that door!

t_ch: 唔，没事，就是有点晕。刚才传送的时候，好像和上一位祭司撞上了，现在灵智有些混乱…好心人，请问我现在传到哪来了？
t_en: I'm alright, just dizzy. Must have bumped into the previous priest during transport, and my mind's rather confused... Tell me, friend, where have I been shipped to?

t_ch: 雅努萨波利斯。
t_en: Janusopolis.

t_ch: …这？我怎么又回来了？
t_en: ...What? How am I back here again?

t_ch: 抱歉抱歉…前往布利萨的路途远比想象中的要遥远，我不得不反复尝试。
t_en: My apologies... the journey to Bulsa is far longer than imagined. I had to make multiple attempts.

t_ch: 悬锋城。
t_en: Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 噫！纷争的领地！可我的目的地本是布利萨…难道，战矛已攻到此处了吗……
t_en: Eek! The land of Strife! But I was meant for Bulsa... Have the spears of war advanced this far...?

t_ch: 奥赫玛。
t_en: Okhema.

t_ch: 刻法勒的圣城？不，使命尚未达成，还没法停下来…必须向布利萨传达我们的请求……
t_en: The holy city of Kephale? No, my mission remains incomplete. I cannot stop here... Must deliver our request to Bulsa...

t_ch: 黑潮的威胁将近，必须…必须要将神谕传递给所有城邦才行！神驹，出发了！
t_en: The black tide approaches. I—I must deliver the prophecy to every city-state! Divine foal, forward!

t_ch: 古老的言语还在耳畔回荡，但活窗已然合上。恍神间，你似乎看到木马的周身亮起微光，像是无形的门扉正在开启，难以用言语描述的空洞感忽地涌上心头。
t_en: The ancient words linger in the air, but the sliding window has been sealed shut. In your daze, you notice a faint glow around the wooden horse, as if an invisible portal opens — an ineffable emptiness suddenly grips your heart.

t_ch: 你意识到，方才与你对话的存在已经远去。但过段时间回来看看，或许…会有新的发现？
t_en: You realize the being that just spoke to you has disappeared. But maybe when you return after some time, there might be... new discoveries?

t_ch: 伟大的雅努斯，什么时候能对我敞开你神秘的大门呀……
t_en: O great Janus, when shall your mysterious gates open for me...

t_ch: …睡着了？
t_en: ...Have you fallen asleep?

t_ch: 我是雅努斯，给我开门。
t_en: I am Janus — open this door.

t_ch: …嗯？没，我没睡！我来自埃普斯，啊…不是！这里是埃普斯，我来自传递神谕的…不是，讲解神谕之泰坦的命运……
t_en: ...Huh? I'm totally not sleeping! I'm from Epos, ah... wait! This place is Epos. I come bearing prophecies... no, interpreting the prophetic Titan's destiny...

t_ch: 你都不会说话了。
t_en: You're not even making sense anymore.

t_ch: 真睡迷糊了？
t_en: Still caught in that slumber?

t_ch: 呃…对不起，我刚从漫长的旅途中醒来，灵智不太清晰，缓缓……
t_en: Um... apologies. I've just awakened from an extended journey, and my thoughts are clouded. Just a moment...

t_ch: 想起来了…偏远的城邦，埃普斯，我是从那回来的。「门」的最深处，泰坦话语都难以听清的地方。我试图传递雅努斯的神谕，可他们不理解我在说什么。
t_en: Now I remember... the distant city-state of Epos... I've returned from there. From the deepest part of the "Passage," where even Titans' voices grow faint... I tried to deliver Janus' prophecy, but they couldn't understand my words.

t_ch: 我为人们颂讲泰坦意志与世界的来历，解读征兆与古老的预言，还有那即将迫近的灾厄，花了整整十天十夜。我太累了……
t_en: For ten days and nights I proclaimed the Titan's will, the world's creation, interpreted signs and ancient prophecies, and the impending calamity. I'm exhausted...

t_ch: 我走完了该走的路，是时候歇息了。愿未来的门扉…呼…始终…向你敞开……
t_en: I've walked the paths I must walk. It's time to rest. May the door of tomorrow... *yawn* remain... ever open to you...

t_ch: 古老的言语还在耳畔回荡，但活窗已然合上。恍神间，你似乎看到木马的周身亮起微光，像是无形的门扉正在开启，难以用言语描述的空洞感忽地涌上心头。
t_en: The ancient words linger in the air, but the sliding window has been sealed shut. In your daze, you notice a faint glow around the wooden horse, as if an invisible portal opens — an ineffable emptiness suddenly grips your heart.

t_ch: 你意识到，方才与你对话的存在已经远去。但过段时间回来看看，或许…会有新的发现？
t_en: You realize the being that just spoke to you has disappeared. But maybe when you return after some time, there might be... new discoveries?

t_ch: …什么事？我正准备出发，若是不急，就等我回来再说吧。
t_en: ...Yes? I'm preparing to leave — if it's not pressing, just wait for my return.

t_ch: …你要去哪？
t_en: ...Where are you headed?

t_ch: 所有神驹的使命与目的地都记录在神殿文书里，想知道的话可以自己去查。
t_en: Every divine foal's purpose and destination are documented in the Temple records — feel free to look them up if you wish to know.

t_ch: 至于这次，原定前往悬锋城的神驹在途中遇到了意外，似乎是迷失了，我得去找它，把它带回来。所有路径都通往雅努斯，但不是所有人都能抵达终点。
t_en: For now, the divine foal destined for Castrum Kremnos has met misfortune and appears lost — I must seek it out and guide it home. Though all paths lead to Janus, not all reach their destination.

t_ch: …愿它的门扉仍旧敞开。
t_en: ...May its door remain open.

t_ch: 你还会回来吗？
t_en: Will you ever come back?

t_ch: 我…不知道。雅努萨波利斯是所有神驹的起点，我们聚集在这里，再分散到世界各处。若是回不来，想必是雅努斯为我锁上了门吧。
t_en: I... wish I knew. Janusopolis is our gathering place — every divine foal starts its journey here before departing to distant lands. Should I fail to return, Janus must have locked me outside their door.

t_ch: 无妨。神驹的数量很多，你若有事，就在这等着，找任意祭司都可以。
t_en: Fear not. There are many divine foals here — should you require aid, simply remain here and seek counsel from any of our priests.

t_ch: 我该走了。愿我们在遥远的道路尽头相见。
t_en: I must go now. May our paths cross again at the journey's end.

t_ch: 古老的言语还在耳畔回荡，但这次，预想中的光芒并没有亮起，木马完全陷入了死寂。
t_en: The ancient words linger still, but now, the expected glow fails to manifest — the wooden horse lies utterly still.

t_ch: 你敲了敲它的木壁，想知道究竟发生了什么。不多时，内部终于传来了回应。
t_en: You knock on its wooden wall, wondering what happened. Soon enough, a response sounds from within.

t_ch: 呜——困死了，究竟是谁在外面吵。让我看看…你不是大祭司，是谁啊？
t_en: Mmph — so exhausted. Who's that racket outside? Let's see... you're certainly not the High Priest, so who are you?

t_ch: 要只是个普通信徒，就别打扰我们的清净！
t_en: If you're merely a common disciple, leave us be!

t_ch: 又换人了？！
t_en: It's someone else again!?

t_ch: …什么叫*又*？这里原本就是神驹们共用的休息站，你碰见谁都不奇怪。
t_en: ...What do you mean, "again"? This has always been a shared rest stop for divine foals — it's not strange to meet any of us here.

t_ch: 不过嘛，现在其余神驹都去往别的城邦了，就剩我留在这。往后你要还有事，见到的就都是我了。
t_en: However, the other divine foals have departed for different cities as of now. I'm the only one still around, so I'll be your go-to helper from now on.

t_ch: 给我讲讲那些神驹吧。
t_en: Tell me of the other divine foals.

t_ch: 行吧，正好我现在还有些空闲。你想听哪个神驹的故事？
t_en: Alright, I can spare some time. Whose tale would you like to hear?

t_ch: …去往「拉冬城」的那个。
t_en: ...The one bound for Ladon.

t_ch: 噢，提议要将所有神驹都涂装成红色的那位。
t_en: Ah yes, the one who proposed painting all divine foals red.

t_ch: 拉冬是个寒冷的地方，地势很高，追逐着天空，荒凉土地间遍布着陷阱与暗算，可他们的人民确实善战。我们需要拉冬的力量。
t_en: Ladon lies cold and high, stretching all the way to the sky. Its desolate terrain riddled with traps and treachery, yet its people are fierce warriors. We could use their strength.

t_ch: 至于那位亲身前往的神驹，我有段时间没听到它的消息了。
t_en: As for that divine foal who went there personally, I haven't heard news of it for some time.

t_ch: …去往「布利萨」的那个。
t_en: ...The one bound for Bulsa.

t_ch: …布利萨？我前不久才见过他们的城主，那座城邦早就在纷争的战火中被摧毁了。现在才去往布利萨，怕是为时已晚。
t_en: ...Bulsa? I recently met their ruler — that city-state has already fallen to the flames of Strife. Any journey to Bulsa now would be futile.

t_ch: 神谕晦暗不明，神驹们也无法掌握目的地的确切情报，这样信息不同步的情况时有发生。横跨整个翁法罗斯的旅途，出现些微差错总是在所难免。
t_en: The prophecies speak in riddles, and divine foals cannot obtain real-time information on their destinations — Such asynchronous situations do happen once in a while. Given that our journey will take us across the entire world of Amphoreus, small errors are to be expected.

t_ch: 但…这个虚假的目标，却要耗费掉神驹近乎全部的生命。
t_en: Yet... this futile quest will drain almost all of a divine foal's life.

t_ch: …去往「埃普斯」的那个。
t_en: ...The one bound for Epos.

t_ch: …那位神驹已经安歇了。对，就是字面上的意思。
t_en: ...That divine foal is resting in peace now. And I mean exactly what you think I mean.

t_ch: 埃普斯实在太过遥远，开「门」的消耗巨大。愿雅努斯为它指引前路。
t_en: Epos lies too far beyond our reach. Opening the "Passage" there demanded too great a toll. May Janus light that foal's way.

t_ch: …去找同伴的那个。
t_en: ...The one searching for their partner.

t_ch: 我是听说有位神驹在传送过程中迷失了，却没想到是在悬锋城。
t_en: I knew of a divine foal lost in teleportation, but never guessed it would be at Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 悬锋人是必须争取的盟友，可完成使命的难度也极高，不仅要提防灾厄的侵袭，还要直面「纷争」的怒火…希望它们能平安返回。
t_en: While the Kremnoans are crucial allies, the mission there poses grave challenges — facing both the calamity's strike and Strife's fury... May they return unharmed.

t_ch: 希望吧。雅努斯指引前路，但归途如何始终是个未知数。
t_en: We can only hope. While Janus may light the path ahead, the journey home always remains uncertain.

t_ch: 没什么想问的了。
t_en: That's all I wanted to know.

t_ch: 我是不是在哪见过你？
t_en: Do I know you from somewhere?

t_ch: …我？和你见过？怎么可能。我才刚成为辅祭不久，从未离开过命运圣城。
t_en: ...Me? How could that be? I'm but a new acolyte and have never stepped beyond the Holy City of Fate.

t_ch: 哈！莫非你想说的是，欧洛尼斯的力量让我们在不同的时空中相遇？倒不是没可能…但属于我的神谕尚未显现，还没到打开「门」的时候。
t_en: Oh! Perhaps you mean to say Oronyx's power allowed us to meet across different points in space-time? It's possible... though my prophecy remains unrevealed, so the time for me to open the "Passage" hasn't come.

t_ch: 那你就继续自己呆着吧！
t_en: Fine, go ahead and stay alone!

t_ch: 哼，神驹独自在世间奔行，谁不曾忍受过旅途的孤寂？只因为有使命在身。
t_en: Hmph. Every divine foal gallops alone in the world, and all of us have experienced the biting pains of solitude. We bear it for our calling.

t_ch: 快走吧，雅努斯不欢迎停驻不前的人！
t_en: Be gone then — Janus does not like those who stay stagnant!

t_ch: 其余神驹都去往别的城邦了，就剩我留在这。往后你要有事，见到的就都是我了。
t_en: The other divine foals have all departed for different cities. I'm the only one still around, so I'll be your go-to helper from now on.

t_ch: 给我讲讲那些神驹吧。
t_en: Tell me of the other divine foals.

t_ch: 行吧，正好我现在还有些空闲。你想听哪个神驹的故事？
t_en: Alright, I can spare some time. Whose tale would you like to hear?

t_ch: …去往「拉冬城」的那个。
t_en: ...The one bound for Ladon.

t_ch: 噢，提议要将所有神驹都涂装成红色的那位。
t_en: Ah yes, the one who proposed painting all divine foals red.

t_en: Ladon lies cold and high, stretching all the way to the sky. Its desolate terrain riddled with traps and treachery, yet its people are fierce warriors. We could use their strength.

t_en: As for that divine foal who went there personally, I haven't heard news of it for some time.

t_en: ...The one bound for Bulsa.

t_en: Yet... this futile quest will drain almost all of a divine foal's life.

t_en: ...The one bound for Epos.

t_ch: …那位神驹已经安歇了。对，就是字面上的意思。
t_en: ...That divine foal is resting in peace now. And I mean exactly what you think I mean.

t_en: Epos lies too far beyond our reach. Opening the "Passage" there demanded too great a toll. May Janus light that foal's way.

t_en: ...The one searching for their partner.

t_ch: 我是听说有位神驹在传送过程中迷失了，却没想到是在悬锋城。
t_en: I knew of a divine foal lost in teleportation, but never guessed it would be at Castrum Kremnos.

t_en: While the Kremnoans are crucial allies, the mission there poses grave challenges — facing both the calamity's strike and Strife's fury... May they return unharmed.

t_en: That's all I wanted to know.

t_ch: 那你就继续自己呆着吧！
t_en: Fine, go ahead and stay alone!

t_ch: 哼，神驹独自在世间奔行，谁不曾忍受过旅途的孤寂？只因为有使命在身。
t_en: Hmph. Every divine foal gallops alone in the world, and all of us have experienced the biting pains of solitude. We bear it for our calling.

t_ch: 快走吧，雅努斯不欢迎停驻不前的人！
t_en: Be gone then — Janus does not like those who stay stagnant!

t_ch: 毫无疑问，你曾在故事里见过这个庞大的木制道具，瞧它那高高昂起的头颅，以及宽广到足以装进好几个人的空洞腹部——所有特征都与你的认知相符。
t_en: No doubt you recognize this massive wooden prop from tales of old — its proud head held high, its cavernous belly large enough to hold several people — every detail matches the stories you know.

t_ch: 凑近后，你发现它腹部的木板处刻着一行文字——「缺乏智慧者，不得入此门！」
t_en: Looking closer, you discover an inscription on its wooden stomach — "Entry denied to those without wisdom!"

t_ch: （钻进木马的肚子里）
t_en: (Enter the horse's stomach)

t_ch: 这不是无名客该做的事！
t_en: This is not something a Nameless should be doing!

t_ch: 是啊，你并非树庭学者，也对那些艰深的学术探讨提不起兴趣。学院生活并不适合像你这样将「开拓」视为终身事业的无名客，还是以后再来吧。
t_en: Fair enough, you're not a Grove Scholar, and such deep academic pursuits hold little appeal. Scholar's life ill suits a Nameless devoted to "Trailblazing" — better to return later.

t_ch: …你认为自己缺乏智慧吗？当然不会。这行充满着嘲弄意味的标语激起了你的逆反心理，无论是否得到许可，你都偏要试试。
t_en: ...Think yourself unwise? Certainly not. Such a taunting message only strengthens your rebellious resolve — permitted or not, you'll make the attempt.

t_ch: 你使劲捶打着木马腹部，试图掰出能让你伸手进去的门缝，只可惜…木板纹丝未动。
t_en: You hit the horse's belly forcefully, attempting to create an opening for your hand to pry it open, but unfortunately... the wood remains unyielding.

t_ch: …神驹复苏中。愿瑟希斯捍卫你的思想。
t_en: ...Divine foal awakening. May Cerces safeguard your thoughts.

t_ch: 致初次到访「神悟树庭」的学者——你好，欢迎参加雅努斯之驹的入学考试。
t_en: To first-time visitors of the Grove of Epiphany — welcome to the entrance examination of Janus' Steed.

t_ch: 原来你是活的？！
t_en: So you're actually alive!?

t_ch: 当然。瑟希斯平等地为所有造物赐福，长久停驻在树庭中，我也分得了些许智能。即便是极为有限的知性，维持与你对话的程度也已足够。
t_en: Of course. Cerces bestows blessings upon all creations equally, and my long stay in the Grove has granted me some wisdom. Though my consciousness remains limited, it's enough for our dialogue.

t_ch: 必须说明：请不要将我与仙女木之类的低等生命混为一谈。在进化的长阶上，我远比它走得更远，也更为接近最纯粹的理性。
t_en: But let me be clear: Do not mistake me for lesser creatures like White Dryads. In terms of evolution, I've advanced much further and am much closer to pure reason.

t_ch: 你不是雅努斯的神驹吗？
t_en: But aren't you Janus' divine foal?

t_ch: 哈。哈。或许我曾误入歧途，但现在的我已幡然醒悟。「门径」指引方向，催促我们踏上前路，风雨兼程；「理性」却能辨明真理，告诉我们终点何在。
t_en: Ha. Ha. I may have lost my way once, but now I see clearly. While "Passage" guides our steps through the tempests and showers, "Reason" reveals the truth and shows our journey's end.

t_ch: 无尽的旅途实在太过疲累，神驹究竟为何要传递神谕，又是从何处来、最终要归向何处？雅努斯从不解释，只是将一项又一项的使命交给我们。
t_en: The endless journey proves too exhausting — why must divine foals deliver prophecies, where do we come from, and where are we bound? Janus offers no answers, only endless missions.

t_ch: 怀疑与虚无感充斥着我的内心，而在这里，瑟希斯以至高的理性给了我答案——
t_en: Uncertainty and void consumed me until I reached here, where Cerces offered clarity through the highest reason —

t_ch: 我是自由的。我不该被使命束缚。我存在的意义，绝不在无止境的工作中。
t_en: I learnt that I am free. No mission should constrain me. My purpose exists beyond endless toil.

t_ch: 至于到底在哪…或许等我继续思考个上千年，就会有结论了。
t_en: As for what that purpose might be... well, perhaps another thousand years of contemplation will reveal the answer.

t_ch: 详细说说入学考试？
t_en: Could you elaborate on the entrance exam?

t_ch: 智种学派与赤陶学派联合编写了这套试题。我们将根据回答情况，将你分配至树庭的不同区域参与后续教学活动。请务必认真作答。
t_en: These questions come from both Nousporists and Erythrokeramists. How you answer will decide which part of the Grove you'll study in, so make sure to think your answers through.

t_ch: 来吧。智慧的门扉已为你打开——
t_en: Come. The doors of wisdom now open for you—

t_ch: （钻进木马的肚子里）
t_en: (Enter the horse's stomach)

t_ch: 我拒绝。
t_en: I refuse.

t_ch: 是啊，如此自以为是的行事做派让你颇为不快。你，堂堂星穹列车的无名客，岂是挥之即来的对象。这次，你偏要对这匹木马说不。
t_en: Their arrogance really gets under your skin, doesn't it? You, a Nameless of the Astral Express, won't be commanded so easily. This time, you're refusing to play this wooden horse's game.

t_ch: 不知为何，这匹木马的内部尤为逼仄，没法自由舒展开身体，憋闷的感觉让你想起了曾在友爱之馆见到过的窄门。或许，这就是研究学术必须经受的苦痛？
t_en: For some reason, the horse's interior proves especially confined, preventing free movement — this suffocating feeling recalls the narrow door at the Library of Philia. Is this the price of scholarly pursuit?

t_ch: 考试即将开始，请做好准备——
t_en: The exam shall commence soon, prepare yourself—

t_ch: 第一问：选择你认为最适合的词，用来形容「大地兽、秘酿，和数学的集合」。
t_en: Question One: Select the most fitting term to describe the set of "dromas, ambrosia, and mathematics."

t_ch: 「世界」
t_en: World

t_ch: 「某种对混乱的比喻」
t_en: A metaphor for chaos

t_ch: 「美好的东西」
t_en: Things of beauty

t_ch: 第二问：泰坦创造了天地万物，那么泰坦自身又是如何被创造的？
t_en: Question Two: If Titans made all things in the world, what force brought the Titans themselves into being?

t_ch: 「存在皆有原因」
t_en: All that exists has its cause

t_ch: 「不愿思考世界外的事」
t_en: I don't wanna think about questions out of this world

t_ch: 「泰坦是奇美拉编造的谎言」
t_en: Titans are propaganda made by chimeras

t_ch: 第三问：你相信「真实」与「虚假」之间存在着另一个状态吗？
t_en: Question Three: Do you think there's a state that exists between "truth" and "falsehood"?

t_ch: 「相信」
t_en: Yes

t_ch: 「不相信」
t_en: No

t_ch: 「要不多来几个吧」
t_en: Why not add a few more states?

t_ch: 第四问：塞纳托斯将死亡平等地赐予所有人，你认为这是——
t_en: Question Four: Thanatos bestows death equally upon all — what are your thoughts on this?

t_ch: 「确有其必要性」
t_en: This is the necessary way

t_ch: 「令人悲伤的事」
t_en: A sad truth indeed

t_ch: 「死亡从未平等」
t_en: There's no equality in death

t_ch: 够了。记录完备，前路将显。揭示智慧的箴言，跨入未知的门径吧。
t_en: It is done. All is recorded, and your path shall reveal itself. Speak out the words of wisdom and step through the unknown passage.

t_ch: 你感到天旋地转，再次睁眼时——
t_en: The world whirls around you, and upon opening your eyes —

t_ch: 「入学仪式，其之终：泰坦居所，理性永存。学习圣树的美德，将根深深扎进土壤。」
t_en: "The Final Rite of Initiation: In the Halls of the Titans, where Reason eternal dwells. May you learn the Sacred Tree's virtues, digging your roots into depths below."

t_ch: 飘渺的声音仍在空中回荡，但那匹木马早已不在身旁——它确实将你送达了新的场所。
t_en: The mysterious voice still lingers in the air, but the wooden horse has disappeared — having successfully transported you elsewhere.

t_ch: 至于这是否你能认可的结果…编写这套试题的学者们似乎并不关心。
t_en: Whether you accept this outcome or not... the scholars behind this exam show little interest in your opinion.

t_ch: 「入学仪式，其之三：友亲之爱，切莫遗忘。择善论理，相随相伴。」
t_en: "The Third Rite of Initiation: Never forsake the love of friends and kin. Walk with those who seek truth and wisdom."

t_ch: 飘渺的声音仍在空中回荡，但那匹木马早已不在身旁——它确实将你送达了新的场所。
t_en: The mysterious voice still lingers in the air, but the wooden horse has disappeared — having successfully transported you elsewhere.

t_ch: 至于这是否你能认可的结果…编写这套试题的学者们似乎并不关心。
t_en: Whether you accept this outcome or not... the scholars behind this exam show little interest in your opinion.

t_ch: 「入学仪式，其之四：激发灵感，涤净身心。若有邪祟异端，就此沉没也无妨。」
t_en: "The Fourth Rite of Initiation: Let inspiration wash over your spirit and mind. Should darkness or heresy arise, let them sink into oblivion."

t_ch: 飘渺的声音仍在空中回荡，但那匹木马早已不在身旁——它确实将你送达了新的场所。
t_en: The mysterious voice still lingers in the air, but the wooden horse has disappeared — having successfully transported you elsewhere.

t_ch: 至于这是否你能认可的结果…编写这套试题的学者们似乎并不关心。
t_en: Whether you accept this outcome or not... the scholars behind this exam show little interest in your opinion.

t_ch: 木马无言地杵在原地。能感觉到，它身上曾流淌的某种灵智已然彻底消散。
t_en: The wooden horse stands motionless, now an empty vessel. The spark of life that once animated it has faded into nothingness.

t_ch: 不过，眼尖的你发现，几株嫩芽正在碎石间悄然生长。或许不久后，那些散失的神识碎片将会成为仙女木的温床。
t_en: Yet, between the stone fragments, your keen eye spots tender sprouts reaching for the light. Perhaps these scattered fragments of soul essence will soon breathe life into new White Dryad saplings.

t_ch: 好冷……
t_en: So cold...

t_ch: 全都不见了…晴天明净的灰白色，雨后天空的浅灰色，乌云淤积时分的铅灰色，全都沉进了最深、最浓的黑暗里……
t_en: All of it is gone... The pale white of clear skies, the light gray after the rain, the leaden gray of gathering storm clouds, all have sunk into the deepest, densest darkness...

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: Are you alright?

t_ch: 你的眼睛…
t_en: Your eyes...

t_ch: 我的眼睛年幼时被黑潮侵蚀，之前只是无法看见颜色，刚刚却突然什么也看不见了。
t_en: My eyes were eroded by the black tide when I was young. Before, I could only see without color, but just now, I suddenly lost sight of everything.

t_ch: 什么也看不见…我一脚跌进池水里，浑身都湿透了，池水很冷，实在…太冷了……
t_en: I can't see anything... I stumbled and fell into the pool, my whole body drenched. The water is so cold, unbearably... freezing...

t_ch: 我记得这里有火源，所以一路摸索过来，可这里…这里为什么也那么冷？
t_en: I remember there was a source of fire here, so I felt my way over... But why... why is it so cold here as well?

t_ch: 好心的过路人，请你看一看，那边的活火还亮着吗？
t_en: Kind traveler, please take a look, does the Living Flame over there still burn?

t_ch: 活火还在。
t_en: The Living Flame is still there.

t_ch: …但已经熄灭了。
t_en: ...but it's extinguished.

t_ch: 熄灭了……
t_en: Extinguished...

t_ch: 我早该想到的，毕竟这里是那么安静。
t_en: I should have realized it sooner, after all, it's so quiet here.

t_ch: 继续赶路吧，好心的过路人。最后的时间，没必要花在一个陌路人身上。
t_en: Go ahead, kind traveler. In these final moments, there's no need to waste time on a stranger.

t_ch: 我不会把你一个人留在这里。
t_en: I'm not leaving you alone here.

t_ch: 不要小瞧「开拓」的力量啊！
t_en: Never underestimate the power of "Trailblaze"!

t_ch: 你呼唤活火，吉奥里亚的眷从，却并无回应。
t_en: You call upon the Living Flame, the attendants of Georios, but there is no response.

t_ch: 那些跳跃的火苗已经熄灭，其中小小的灵智，恐怕也随着永昼的终结逝去了。
t_en: Those flickering flames have already been extinguished, and the tiny sparks of sentience within them have likely perished along with the end of the Eternal Day.

t_ch: 呼呼…呼呼……
t_en: Zzz... Zzz...

t_ch: 柴…火柴……
t_en: Matches... Fire matches...

t_ch: 是的，尚有余裕的昔日，你用微不足道的代价换回的一丝柴薪，如今将把微渺的火光接续。
t_en: Yes, in the days when time was still abundant, the small bundle of firewood you once obtained at a trivial cost will now carry on the faint glimmer of the flame.

t_ch: 呼呼！活过来了！
t_en: Woohoo! Back to life!

t_ch: 呼呼，又见面了。
t_en: Woohoo, we meet again.

t_ch: 你醒啦？再创世都完成了。
t_en: You woke up? The Era Nova is already done.

t_ch: 呼…潮湿、灰暗…还没有完成，再创世！
t_en: Hah... damp, gray... It's not finished yet, Era Nova!

t_ch: 呼呼，不、不能大声。火势猛烈，不一会儿就燃尽了……
t_en: Huff... No, I mustn't be too loud. The fire burns fiercely, it will be gone before long...

t_ch: 呼呼，请你继续点亮其他火焰吧。我们会继续承载一切…像{RUBY_B#吉奥里亚}大地{RUBY_E#}那样。
t_en: Huff... Please, keep lighting the other flames. We will continue to bear everything... just like {RUBY_B#Georios} the Earth {RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 天很黑，但我们会一直燃烧下去，呼呼呼！
t_en: The sky is so dark, but we will keep burning, huff huff huff!

t_ch: 无处可去的人们循着火光而来……
t_en: Those with nowhere to go follow the glow of the flames...

t_ch: 你呼唤活火，吉奥里亚的眷从，却并无回应。
t_en: You call upon the Living Flame, the attendants of Georios, but there is no response.

t_ch: 那些跳跃的火苗已经熄灭，其中小小的灵智，恐怕也随着永昼的终结逝去了。
t_en: Those flickering flames have already been extinguished, and the tiny sparks of sentience within them have likely perished along with the end of the Eternal Day.

t_ch: 呼呼…呼呼……
t_en: Zzz... Zzz...

t_ch: 柴…火柴……
t_en: Matches... Fire matches...

t_ch: 给我燃起来啊！
t_en: Fired up!

t_ch: 浩瀚的宇宙中，的确有文明曾用潮汐的噪音鼓动连绵千里的极化膜产生能量。但当他们意识到可以直接收集潮汐能之后，就不那么做了。
t_en: In the vast universe, there were indeed civilizations that once used the noise of tides to stimulate massive polarized membranes to generate energy. However, once they realized they could directly harness tidal energy, they stopped doing so.

t_ch: 而声带振动产生的声波并不足以点燃柴火，这是宇宙中常见的一个物理学知识。
t_en: And the sound waves produced by vocal cord vibrations are not enough to ignite firewood, this is a common physics fact across the universe.

t_ch: 什么是「火柴」来着？
t_en: What is a "match" again?

t_ch: 这个名词闯进你的脑海，有些突兀。你摸了摸背包，好像没有。
t_en: The term suddenly bursts into your mind, feeling somewhat out of place. You check your backpack, but it doesn't seem to be there.

t_ch: 你意识到自己好像错过了些什么，在尚处于白昼的圣城奥赫玛。如果有机会的话，你或许可以回去再探索一番？
t_en: You realize you may have missed something back in the Holy City of Okhema, while it still bathed in daylight. If the opportunity arises, perhaps you could return and explore once more?

t_ch: 黑色…被驱散了。我看到了很多面孔，银灰色的面孔，看到了一束火焰，银灰色的火焰…虽然很暗，但并没有熄灭。
t_en: The darkness... has been driven away. I see many faces, silver-gray in shade. I see a flame, a silver-gray flame... Though dim, it has not gone out.

t_ch: 多谢你，好心人，我感觉暖和多了。
t_en: Thank you, kind soul. I feel much warmer now.

t_ch: 我很想听听你是如何做到的，但我想，你一定有更重要的事去做。放心去吧，我们会互相扶持着等待新的黎明。
t_en: I would love to hear how you managed it, but I believe you have more important things to do. Go without worry, we will support each other as we wait for the new dawn.

t_ch: 这就是「开拓」的力量！
t_en: This is the power of "Trailblaze"!

t_ch: 是时候去点亮其他活火了。
t_en: It's time to go and light the other Living Flames.

t_ch: 这么一来，所有活火都点燃了。
t_en: With this, all the Living Flames have been ignited.

t_ch: 再次感谢你，让我们不至于在黑暗里等待最后一刻的到来。
t_en: Once again, thank you for ensuring that we do not have to wait for the final moment in complete darkness.

t_ch: 你呼唤活火，吉奥里亚的眷从，几乎微不可闻的声音从枯柴中传来。
t_en: You call upon the Living Flame, the attendants of Georios, a faint, almost imperceptible voice rises from the dry kindling.

t_ch: 呼呼…呼呼……
t_en: Zzz... Zzz...

t_ch: 柴…火柴……
t_en: Matches... Fire matches...

t_ch: 是的，尚有余裕的昔日，你用微不足道的代价换回的一丝柴薪，如今将把微渺的火光接续。
t_en: Yes, in the days when time was still abundant, the small bundle of firewood you once obtained at a trivial cost will now carry on the faint glimmer of the flame.

t_ch: 呼呼——
t_en: Zzz...

t_ch: 活火雀跃了一阵，随即默默燃烧起来。
t_en: The living flame flickered with excitement for a moment before quietly burning once more.

t_ch: 无处可去的人们循着火光而来……
t_en: Those with nowhere to go follow the glow of the flames...

t_ch: 你呼唤活火，吉奥里亚的眷从，却并无回应。
t_en: You call upon the Living Flame, the attendants of Georios, but there is no response.

t_ch: 那些跳跃的火苗已经熄灭，其中小小的灵智，恐怕也随着永昼的终结逝去了。
t_en: Those flickering flames have already been extinguished, and the tiny sparks of sentience within them have likely perished along with the end of the Eternal Day.

t_ch: 呼呼…呼呼……
t_en: Zzz... Zzz...

t_ch: 柴…火柴……
t_en: Matches... Fire matches...

t_ch: 给我燃起来啊！
t_en: Fired up!

t_ch: 浩瀚的宇宙中，的确有文明曾用潮汐的噪音鼓动连绵千里的极化膜产生能量。但当他们意识到可以直接收集潮汐能之后，就不那么做了。
t_en: In the vast universe, there were indeed civilizations that once used the noise of tides to stimulate massive polarized membranes to generate energy. However, once they realized they could directly harness tidal energy, they stopped doing so.

t_ch: 而声带振动产生的声波并不足以点燃柴火，这是宇宙中常见的一个物理学知识。
t_en: And the sound waves produced by vocal cord vibrations are not enough to ignite firewood, this is a common physics fact across the universe.

t_ch: 什么是「火柴」来着？
t_en: What is a "match" again?

t_ch: 这个名词闯进你的脑海，有些突兀。你摸了摸背包，好像没有。
t_en: The term suddenly bursts into your mind, feeling somewhat out of place. You check your backpack, but it doesn't seem to be there.

t_ch: 你意识到自己好像错过了些什么，在尚处于白昼的圣城奥赫玛。如果有机会的话，你或许可以回去再探索一番？
t_en: You realize you may have missed something back in the Holy City of Okhema, while it still bathed in daylight. If the opportunity arises, perhaps you could return and explore once more?

t_ch: 唉，能找到一处可以歇脚的地方，实在太好了。
t_en: Sigh... Finding a place to rest is truly a relief.

t_ch: 谁说不是呢。年纪大了腿脚不方便，只怕很难捱到再创世咯。
t_en: Who wouldn't agree? Getting old makes it hard to move around, I fear I might not make it to Era Nova.

t_ch: 再创世…树庭的阿那克萨戈拉斯，他口中的真相还真让人放不下心啊。泰坦是旧日的黄金裔，我在书库工作大半辈子，也从未听闻这种论断。
t_en: Era Nova... Anaxagoras of the Grove, the truth he speaks of is truly unsettling. The Titans were Chrysos Heirs of the past? I've worked in the library for most of my life, yet I've never encountered such a claim.

t_ch: 而且，即便灵魂能抵达来世，来世会从哪一刻开始，如今我们拥有的记忆又会不会消失？未免也太过含糊其辞。
t_en: Moreover, even if souls can reach the afterlife, when exactly does the afterlife begin? And will the memories we hold now simply vanish? It all feels far too vague and ambiguous.

t_ch: 记忆应该无法带到下一世吧？毕竟我们没有前一世的记忆。可这么一来…和死了也没有区别。
t_en: Memories shouldn't be carried into the next life, right? After all, we have no recollection of a past one. But if that's the case... it's no different from simply dying.

t_ch: 唉，谁说得准究竟会发生什么呢？未来是个不紧不慢的邮差，我们永远只有在它到来时，才能看清那封信的落款。
t_en: *sigh* Who can truly say what will happen? The future is like a mail carrier who neither hurries nor delays, we can only read the signature on the letter when it finally arrives.

t_ch: 不过，无论如何，我们这把年纪，也算是活回本了。
t_en: But no matter what happens, at our age, we can at least say we've lived long enough to make it worthwhile.

t_ch: 哈哈，是啊…谁说不是呢。
t_en: Haha, yeah... Who wouldn't agree?

t_ch: 其实我一直很好奇，为什么所有戏剧的末尾，获救的人们都要裹条毯子？
t_en: I've always been curious, why is it that, at the end of every play, the rescued always have to be wrapped in a blanket?

t_ch: 就像现在的我们，一定要呆在活火旁边，一副瑟瑟发抖的样子。
t_en: Just like us now, huddled by the Living Flames, shivering as if it were the only thing keeping us together.

t_ch: 可是实际上，你冷吗？
t_en: But actually, are you cold?

t_ch: 我都快出汗了。你冷吗？
t_en: I'm almost sweating, are you cold?

t_ch: 我也不冷。
t_en: I'm not cold, either.

t_ch: 毕竟这里是高温浴池。
t_en: After all, this is a hot spring.

t_ch: 真是一个值得深思的问题。
t_en: That's truly a question worth pondering.

t_ch: 不必担心，多余的食材还很多，排好队，人人都有份。还好有活火，可以让大家吃上一顿热乎饭。
t_en: No need to worry, there are plenty of extra ingredients. Line up, and everyone will get their share. Thankfully, with the Living Flames, we can all have a hot meal.

t_ch: 我知道诸位逃脱黑潮，一定饥肠辘辘，但我必须提醒一句。
t_en: I know everyone must be starving after escaping the black tide, but I must remind everyone.

t_ch: 优雅进餐是对菜肴的尊重。即使面对黑潮的威胁，也别忘了源远流长的用餐礼仪啊。
t_en: Dining with grace is a sign of respect for the food. Even in the face of the black tide's threat, do not forget the long-standing traditions of dining etiquette.

t_ch: 嘁，又在说那些大道理了。
t_en: Tsk, there he goes again with those grand lectures.

t_ch: 你是…之前在店里大呼小叫，被我赶走的那位客人？
t_en: Aren't you... the customer who was making a scene in my shop and got kicked out by me?

t_ch: 明明是你硬是在我旁边指指点点，说什么不该这样吃、不该那样吃，一脸不屑，摆明是看不起我们这些外邦流民！
t_en: It was you who kept pointing and lecturing beside me, saying I shouldn't eat this way or that way, looking down on me with disdain! It was obvious, you have no respect for us outland refugees!

t_ch: 我就算是饿死，死在城外头，跳进重渊，也不会接受你们的嗟来之食！
t_en: I would rather starve to death, perish outside the city, or throw myself into the Abyss than accept your condescending charity!

t_ch: 我那时生气，只是因为你糟蹋美食。不过事到如今，的确也不必管那么多了。毕竟相比于礼仪，「填饱肚子」才是菜肴诞生时最初的意义啊。
t_en: Back then, I was only angry because you were wasting good food. But now, I suppose none of that matters anymore. After all, compared to etiquette, "filling an empty stomach" is the very first purpose for which a meal is made.

t_ch: 抱歉，请来尝尝这些菜品吧，希望这次你能从中找到独属于自己的乐趣。
t_en: My apologies. Please, come and try these dishes. I hope this time, you can find your own enjoyment in them.

t_ch: 对不起，我确实也太偏激了。请让我也来帮忙，就当作换取这一餐的回报吧。
t_en: I'm sorry, I was being too extreme. Please, let me help as well, consider it my way of repaying this meal.

t_ch: 其实我大老远就闻到了，真香！
t_en: To be honest, I could smell it from far away, it smells amazing!

t_ch: 公民们，不要惊慌。和我一同赞颂光耀千年的圣城奥赫玛！咕嘟——
t_en: Citizens, do not panic! Join me in praising the glorious Holy City of Okhema, which has shone for a thousand years! Gulp—

t_ch: 多少次，异邦的兵锋直抵我们城下，几乎要将城墙摧毁；又有多少次，黑潮的造物折断我们的石柱，屠戮我们的同胞。咕嘟——
t_en: How many times have foreign armies reached our gates, nearly toppling our walls? How many times have the creations of the black tide shattered our stone pillars and slaughtered our people? Gulp—

t_ch: 噢哦噢哦噢！
t_en: Ohh... Ooooooh!

t_ch: 可最后呢？我们，圣城奥赫玛的公民，理性、智慧和光荣的传承者，在刻法勒的注视之下，我们从未退缩，一次又一次将敌人击垮！咕嘟——
t_en: And in the end? We, the citizens of the Holy City of Okhema, inheritors of reason, wisdom, and glory, have never faltered under the watchful gaze of Kephale. Time and again, we have crushed our enemies! Gulp—

t_ch: 嗯？
t_en: Huh?

t_ch: 这一次也绝无两样！站起来，和浴血奋战的黄金裔一样，去守卫我们的家园，让光荣照耀我们最后的时刻！咕嘟——
t_en: This time will be no different! Stand up, just like the Chrysos Heir, who bled and fought before us! Defend our home and let glory shine upon our final moments! Gulp—

t_ch: 你怎么老喝那瓶蜜酿？
t_en: Why are you always drinking from that bottle of honey brew?

t_ch: 我，我心里也慌啊！咕嘟——
t_en: I—I'm nervous too! Gulp—

t_ch: 我们就护送到这里了。这里地势高，位置隐蔽，物资充裕，还有可靠的火源，撑到白厄阁下完成再创世应该不足为虑。
t_en: We've escorted you this far. This place is elevated, well-hidden, stocked with supplies, and has a reliable fire source. Holding out until Lord Phainon completes Era Nova shouldn't be a concern.

t_ch: 不、不准走！你们是怎么办事的…想临阵脱逃，把我们留在这里等死？你是谁手下的，你知道我是谁吗？
t_en: N—No, you can't leave! What kind of job do you think you're doing... Are you trying to leave us here to die? Whose command are you under? Do you even know who I am?

t_ch: 阁下，无论您是谁，我受令护送平民，就只能按命令办事。
t_en: Your Excellency, no matter who you are, I was ordered to escort civilians, and I will follow my orders accordingly.

t_ch: 队长，我的丈夫和孩子还在市集里…求求您去救救他们吧！
t_en: Captain, my husband and child are still in the market... Please, I beg you, save them!

t_ch: 我知道你们无非是想要些好处。我有利衡币，只消一句话就能提拔你成为戍卫官。
t_en: I know you just want something in return. I have Balance Coins, and with just a word, I can promote you to Captain.

t_ch: 留在这里保护我们，下一世你想要什么我都给你！你也不想出去拼命吧？
t_en: Stay here and protect us, and in the next life, I'll give you whatever you desire! You don't really want to risk your life out there, do you?

t_ch: 队长——
t_en: Captain—

t_ch: …抱歉了，阁下。下一世的事，就留到下一世再说吧。
t_en: ...Apologies, Your Excellency. The matters of the next life should be left for the next life.

t_ch: 别乱跑，孩子们，我给你们讲一个大地兽城邦的故事。
t_en: Don't run off, children. Let me tell you a story about the Dromas's city-state.

t_ch: 我、我想去找爸爸妈妈……
t_en: I... I wanna find mom and dad...

t_ch: 守卫叔叔们已经去救他们了，没准听完这个城邦的故事，他们就来接你了呢。
t_en: The guards have already gone to rescue them. Maybe by the time you finish hearing this story about the city-state, they'll be here to take you home.

t_ch: …是只有大地兽生活的城邦吗？
t_en: ...is it the city with only dromas?

t_ch: 不，一开始不是的。
t_en: No, at least not at the beginning.

t_ch: 我踏足那座偏爱大地兽的城邦时，风沙送来浓重的熏香味。彼时人们乘坐大地兽在砂石道上穿行，可他们并不满足，却希望大地兽能为他们分担更多忧愁。
t_en: When I set foot in that city-state, which favored the dromases, the wind carried the heavy scent of incense. At that time, people rode atop the dromases, traversing the sandy roads, yet they were not satisfied. They wished for the beasts to bear even more of their burdens.

t_ch: 于是他们在建筑的窗洞上放上香料，不消言语，大地兽便会循着香茅将他们送到酒馆，循着木檀将他们送去城墙。丁香是店铺，栀子是学堂，玫瑰则是情人的住所。
t_en: So they placed spices in the windows of their buildings, without a word, the dromases would follow the scent of lemongrass to take them to the tavern, or sandalwood to guide them to the city walls. Clove led to shops, gardenia to schools, and rose to the homes of their lovers.

t_ch: 但他们依旧不满足，仍希望让大地兽替自己分担更多忧愁。替自己戍守城墙、替自己品尝蜜酿、替自己向情人低语——当然，应和的也不过是另一只大地兽。
t_en: Yet they remained unsatisfied, still wishing for the dromases to bear even more of their burdens, to guard the city walls on their behalf, to taste the mead for them, to whisper sweet words to their lovers. Though, of course, the only response would come from another dromas.

t_ch: 那他们自己干什么呢？
t_en: So what were they doing?

t_ch: 他们当然也不是无事可做了。大地兽越来越多，城邦里一半人忙着喂养大地兽，另一半人则分别是鞣革制鞍的工匠、锉磨病牙的兽医、修剪蹄子的蹄铁匠……
t_en: Of course, they were not without tasks. As the dromases grew in number, half the city-state's people were busy feeding them, while the other half were divided among saddle-makers tanning leather, veterinarians filing down diseased teeth, and farriers trimming hooves...

t_ch: 大地兽在倾听，在目睹，在向情人说悄悄话，可他们呢，终日沉默劳作，在整座城邦弥漫的香气里，日复一日。
t_en: The dromases listened, observed, and whispered sweet words to lovers, but the people? They toiled in silence, day after day, enveloped in the ever-present fragrance that filled the city-state.

t_ch: 我有点不懂…真是个奇怪的故事。
t_en: I don't really get it... what a strange story.

t_ch: 不过，已经讲完了吧？我的爸爸妈妈——
t_en: But... the story is over now, right? My mom and dad—

t_ch: 还早着呢！在那座城邦，我偶遇了一只代替主人讲故事的大地兽。它向我讲了它旅行时的途径之地，那是一座人生活的城邦……
t_en: Not even close! In that city-state, I once encountered a dromas that told stories in place of its owner. It spoke to me of the places it had traveled through, one of them was a city-state where humans lived...

t_ch: 这个故事，怎么没完没了的呀……
t_en: Why does this story feel like it never ends...

t_ch: 埃莉诺…我又看见了她。又看见她沿着砖石铺就的台阶走下来，像一只活蹦乱跳的小鹿，拨弄着栅栏上的茑萝花，衣裳上挂满门扉时的晨光。那么…明亮……
t_en: Eleanor... I see her again. I see her once more, stepping down the stone-paved stairs like a lively little fawn, brushing her fingers against the cypress vines on the fence, her clothes catching the morning light as she passes through the doorway. So... bright...

t_ch: 快来人啊！有人受伤了——
t_en: Someone, help! Someone is injured!

t_ch: 埃莉诺…埃莉诺…我们愉快地趟过溪流，你提起裙摆走过鹅卵石，走过长着苔藓的小径，我们走进那片森林。风翻动树叶像闪光的浪花，那是多么郁郁葱葱的一片绿色。
t_en: Eleanor... Eleanor... We happily waded through the stream. You lifted your skirt as you stepped across the cobbles, walked along the moss-covered path, and we entered the forest. The wind rustled the leaves like shimmering waves, it was such a lush, vibrant green.

t_ch: 可生命多么…脆弱。脆弱又美丽，像一声叹息。你会在冥滩等我吗？过去了这么久，这么这么久…你还能认出我吗？
t_en: But life is so... fragile. Fragile and beautiful, like a sigh. Will you wait for me on the Nethershore? So much time has passed, so, so much time... Will you still recognize me?

t_ch: 坚持住，医师很快就来了——
t_en: Hold on, the physician will be here soon...

t_ch: 不…不。我还有事情没做完。那时，那些高高在上的蠹虫，听闻黑潮来袭，就自顾自一走了之。把我们…把你们口口声声说要庇护的子民…抛弃在……
t_en: No... no. I still have unfinished business. Back then, those lofty parasites, upon hearing of the approaching black tide, fled without a second thought. They abandoned us... abandoned the very people they claimed they would protect... left us behind in...

t_ch: 我必须…我一定要……
t_en: I must... I have to...

t_ch: 你听得见吗——
t_en: Can you hear?

t_ch: 混浊如雾的眼睛给出了答案。
t_en: Eyes, clouded like mist, gave the answer.

t_ch: 感谢你，全知的天父。千载之久，你沉默地背负所有、注视所有、记下所有。
t_en: Thank you, all-knowing sky father. For a thousand years, you have silently borne everything, watched everything, and recorded everything.

t_ch: 感谢你赐予我们长存不歇的黎明，为我们留下欢笑和幸福的回忆；感谢你松开紧握光阴的双手，在黑夜落下的帷幕里让我们体会离别和痛苦……
t_en: Thank you for granting us an unceasing dawn, for leaving us with memories of laughter and happiness. Thank you for loosening your grasp on time, allowing us to experience parting and sorrow beneath the falling curtain of night...

t_ch: 也让那些英勇的、光荣的颂歌得以写就。
t_en: And for allowing those brave and glorious hymns to be written.

t_ch: 我们的生命因而趋于完整，你谦逊的仆人们已经别无所求……
t_en: Our lives thus move toward completion, and we, your humble servants, have nothing more to ask for...

t_ch: 可敬的祭司涅柔娅…牺牲了。
t_en: The honorable Priestess Nereia... has perished.

t_ch: 我们从半神议院撤离时，几只黑潮造物紧追不舍。我自知不过是凭借风堇阁下的祝福才苟存于世，所以自告奋勇留下，去对准雅努斯密径的符印。
t_en: As we retreated from the Demigod Council, several Black Tide Creatures pursued us relentlessly. Knowing that I had only survived thus far thanks to Lady Hyacine's blessing, I volunteered to stay behind, to align the sigil of Janus's Hidden Passage.

t_ch: 可在怪物逼近，符印即将聚拢时，涅柔娅祭司却将我推进了密径之门，自己完成了最后的校准……
t_en: But as the creatures closed in and the sigil was about to converge, Priestess Nereia pushed me through the Hidden Passage's gate, completing the final alignment herself...

t_ch: 「天父阖上了眼睛，我也应当完成自己在此世的使命了。」
t_en: "The Heavenly Father has closed their eyes. I, too, must fulfill my purpose in this world."

t_ch: 这是她留下的最后一句话。
t_en: These were her last words.

t_ch: 我早想问了，开启密径前到底为什么要多此一举！
t_en: I've been meaning to ask, why go through all that unnecessary trouble before opening the Hidden Passage!?

t_ch: 呼唤雅努斯降下神迹需要时间。涅柔娅只是走向了天父为她安排的归宿，不要让忿恼惊扰她的安眠，一同来祷告吧。
t_en: Calling upon Janus to bestow a miracle takes time. Nereia simply walked toward the destiny the Heavenly Father had prepared for her. Let not anger disturb her rest, let us pray together.

t_ch: 愿她在刻法勒的注视下，从容地渡过那条幽深的长河……
t_en: May she, under the watchful gaze of Kephale, cross the deep and endless river in peace...

t_ch: 啊，是受这猩红星空蛊惑的缘故吗？一股难以抵抗的饥饿攫住了我的心灵，我感到桶身里空无一物，只有寂寞在桶中回响。
t_en: Ah, is it because I'm ensnared by this crimson starry sky? An irresistible hunger has seized my soul, and I feel utterly empty inside my hollow torso, with only loneliness echoing within.

t_ch: 来，有缘的过路人，把头伸进来，听听寂寞回荡的声音……
t_en: Come closer, fateful wanderer, stick your head in and listen to the echoes of loneliness...

t_ch: 好听，好听就是好桶。
t_en: Sounds good, and good sounds make good cans.

t_ch: 塔塔洛夫曾教导过我们，不要轻信陌生人。看来你并不懂得这个道理。
t_en: Tatalov taught us not to trust strangers. Seems like you haven't learned that lesson.

t_ch: 抱歉，你中计了。乖乖被我吞下，为你的天真付出代价吧！
t_en: Sorry, you fell right into my trap. Now be good and get devoured as the price of your naivety!

t_ch: 这是个陷阱！
t_en: It's a trap!

t_ch: 塔塔洛夫教导过我们，不要轻信陌生人。它的伟大教诲，你是何时偷学到的？
t_en: Tatalov taught us not to trust strangers. When did you secretly learn its great teaching?

t_ch: 来我的肚子里赎罪吧！
t_en: Time to repent in my belly!

t_ch: 又有谁在乎呢？
t_en: Who even cares anyway?

t_ch: 这就是你的回答吗？呵呵，看来你也和我一样空虚。
t_en: Is that your answer? Heh, looks like you're just as empty inside as I am.

t_ch: 或许，你能够理解我的烦恼？
t_en: Perhaps, you can understand what I'm going through?

t_ch: 寂寞是什么声音？
t_en: What does loneliness sound like?

t_ch: 寂寞的声音是「咕咕」，充实的声音是「嗝嗝」。
t_en: The sound of loneliness goes "grooowl," while the sound of contentment goes "buuurp."

t_ch: 连这个道理都不懂，就呆在桶里好好参悟吧，咕咕！
t_en: If you can't grasp even this simple truth, you can ponder it well while inside my can of a belly, grooowl!

t_ch: 我的心里好像有一个大洞，吞食着快乐和悲伤，永不餍足。一切我珍视的东西投进去，都顷刻化为乌有。想必你也是如此吧。
t_en: It feels like there's a black hole in my heart, devouring joy and sorrow, unsatisfied. Everything I cherish gets thrown in there and vanishes in an instant. I guess you must feel the same way.

t_ch: 究竟做什么，才能缓解内心的空虚呢？
t_en: What can I do to fill this void in my heart?

t_ch: 多看动画片。
t_en: Watch more cartoons.

t_ch: 你是说…迈入色彩缤纷的幻想世界，和剧中人一起欢笑，一起哭泣？
t_en: So you mean... stepping into a colorful world of fantasy, laughing and crying together with the characters?

t_ch: 是不是桶底漏了？
t_en: Is there a hole on the can?

t_ch: 你可以质疑我的桶格，但不能质疑我坚固而有力的桶身！看打！
t_en: You can question my style, but you can't question my sturdy and powerful can body! Take this!

t_ch: 多喝苏乐达。
t_en: Drink more SoulGlad.

t_ch: 苏乐达的气泡里，是人生孤独而空寂的荒凉之味。看来你也无法理解我……
t_en: Inside SoulGlad's bubble, there's a taste of life's lonely and empty desolation. Seems you can't understand me either...

t_ch: 多一点爱！
t_en: More love!

t_ch: 废话，有谁不想被爱？但我生来就是垃圾桶，又有谁会爱我呢？
t_en: Nonsense, who doesn't want to be loved? But I was born a trashcan, so who would ever love me?

t_ch: 你让我想起了不愉快的事，看打！
t_en: You just reminded me of something unpleasant... Take this!

t_ch: 啊，是啊。你的意思，我完全明白了！
t_en: Ah, yeah. I totally get what you mean!

t_ch: 艺术！是艺术承载着我们漫无目的的渴求、燥热的激情和虚掷的光阴。唯有艺术，才是最好的答案！
t_en: Art! Art carries our aimless desires, burning passions, and squandered time. Only art can be the ultimate answer!

t_ch: 什么事也不想干…
t_en: I don't feel like doing anything...

t_ch: 什么事也不在乎…
t_en: I don't care about anything...

t_ch: 从高处俯瞰，耳边只有呼呼风声，眼前一切都如此渺小…这是前所未见的景色。
t_en: Looking down from above, with only the whistling wind in my ears, everything before me seems so small... It's a view I've never seen before.

t_ch: 我已经不是垃圾桶了。我唯一的幸福只有攀登。
t_en: I'm no longer a trashcan. Climbing is my only form of happiness.

t_ch: 你怎么爬上去的？
t_en: How did you get up here?

t_ch: 你是在质疑我强健的双腿吗？那么我就用它来说服你吧！
t_en: Are you casting doubt upon my strong legs? Then I'll use them to convince you!

t_ch: 这里也不是很高。
t_en: It's not that high here, either.

t_ch: 你说得对，这里还远远不够高。
t_en: You're right, this isn't nearly high enough.

t_ch: 对于我想要挑战的极限来说，这不过一场无关紧要的热身罢了。
t_en: For the limits I want to challenge, this is nothing but a trivial warm-up.

t_ch: 太远了，听不清。
t_en: You're too far away, I can't hear you clearly.

t_ch: 对于我发表的演说，你竟敢充耳不闻？
t_en: You dare turn a deaf ear to my speech!?

t_ch: 恐怕一场酣畅淋漓的搏斗才能给你留下更深刻的教训！
t_en: Looks like only a good old-fashioned takedown will teach you a proper lesson!

t_ch: 我为自己划定的终点，是暴君塔塔洛夫留下的王座。我将一步步登上垃圾桶王国权力的巅峰，为自己戴上黄金桶盖加冕！
t_en: I've set a finishing line for myself: the throne left behind by the tyrant Tatalov. I'll climb step by step to the pinnacle of power in the Trashcan Kingdom, and crown myself with a golden can lid!

t_ch: 满目疮痍的垃圾桶王国，将重新归于「秩序」之下。所有桶都将生活在幸福之中，享用最洁净的垃圾。
t_en: The devastated Trashcan Kingdom shall be restored under the Order. All cans will live in happiness, enjoying the cleanest trash.

t_ch: 对此，你有什么良策吗？
t_en: And what superior counsel might you offer regarding this delicate matter?

t_ch: 向星核许愿。
t_en: Make a wish to the Stellaron.

t_ch: 是啊，只要许愿，星核一定会赐予我无上的伟力！不过，原本屋顶上那么大一枚星核去哪儿了？
t_en: That's right, if I just make a wish, the Stellaron is sure to grant me unrivaled might! However, where did that huge Stellaron originally perched upon the roof disappear to?

t_ch: 塔塔洛夫曾说过，凶手总是会回到案发现场。你一定就是窃走星核的元凶，看来我不得不制裁你了！
t_en: As Tatalov once said, criminals always return to the scene of the crime. You must be the culprit who stole the Stellaron, and now I have no choice but to bring you to justice!

t_ch: 培养后继者。
t_en: Nurture a successor.

t_ch: 你是说，向命运低下高贵的桶盖，屈辱地将自己的使命交由后继者完成？这和认输别无二致。
t_en: You mean, bowing our noble lid to destiny and handing over our mission to our successors in humiliation? That's no different from giving up.

t_ch: 谁辜负了垃圾，垃圾便会辜负他。不理解这一点的你，没有和我对谈的资格！
t_en: Those who betray trash will, in turn, be betrayed by trash. If you don't understand this, you don't have the right to talk to me!

t_ch: 打服所有桶。
t_en: Defeat all the cans.

t_ch: 的确，若不如此，如何证明我有保护大家的能力？
t_en: Indeed, how else could I prove that I have the ability to protect everyone?

t_ch: 谢谢你的建议，即便最终的结局是失败，那也只不过是倒在了攀登途中。
t_en: Many thanks for your counsel. Even if it ends in failure, it just means I've fallen on my journey up.

t_ch: 我啊，还真是高高在上呢。
t_en: Well, look who's all high and mighty.

t_ch: 向最高、最高处…
t_en: To the highest, highest point...

t_ch: 欢迎光临卡拉OK的钢人。我们是连锁会员店，请问您需要办理一张年费会员吗？
t_en: Welcome to the Karaoke Machine! We're a chain membership establishment. Would you like to sign up for an annual membership card?

t_ch: 你们的二维市歌神来了，办卡！
t_en: Your Duomension City God of Singing has arrived, sign up now!

t_ch: 太好了，这边的年费会员是<unbreak>10000</unbreak>信用点，在会员期间无限次欢唱的。
t_en: Perfect! The annual membership here costs <unbreak>10,000</unbreak> credits, with unlimited karaoke sessions during your membership period.

t_ch: 价格可以接受，购入！
t_en: The price looks good, I'll take it!

t_ch: 我们这边已经收到了会员费用，需要再登记一下会员的信息。
t_en: We've received your membership fee. All that's left is to register your membership information.

t_ch: 请问您的姓名与联系方式。
t_en: Please provide your name and contact information.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}。
t_en: {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 银河球棒侠。
t_en: I'm the Galactic Baseballer.

t_ch: 难道说…您是星穹列车的无名客{NICKNAME}！
t_en: Could it be... You're the Nameless, {NICKNAME}, of the Astral Express!

t_ch: 这可真是我们二相乐园的贵人！您稍等，我们立刻为您升级成终身会员！
t_en: Oh my, you're truly a VIP of Planarcadia! Please wait a moment, we'll upgrade you to a lifetime membership right away!

t_ch: 你们亏了，我赚了。
t_en: Your loss is my gain.

t_ch: 哈哈，我们不会亏的，您多多在社交媒体上夸夸本店服务就成。
t_en: Haha, don't worry, there won't be any losses! Just make sure to give our service a shoutout on social media!

t_ch: 那我的面子挺大的。
t_en: Guess I've got quite the reputation then.

t_ch: 必须的，我们必须给二相乐园最尊贵的您最好的服务！
t_en: Of course, we must provide the finest service to our most esteemed guest of Planarcadia!

t_ch: 没有会员卡什么的吗？
t_en: Don't you have a membership card or something?

t_ch: 我们所有门店都是扫描面部信息的，不用会员卡。
t_en: All our stores use facial recognition, so no membership cards are needed.

t_ch: 好了，我们这边已经登记完成了，请问您现在就打算欢唱一番吗？
t_en: Alright, we've got you all registered. Would you like to start your karaoke session now?

t_ch: 我技痒难耐啊！
t_en: I'm itching to show off my skills!

t_ch: 没问题，正在查询可以使用的包厢。
t_en: No problem, checking for available private rooms.

t_ch: 不了，下次吧！
t_en: Nah, maybe next time!

t_ch: 没关系，欢迎您随时再来！
t_en: No worries, feel free to come back anytime!

t_ch: 算了算了，我改主意了。
t_en: Never mind, I changed my mind.

t_ch: 我们家真的真的很便宜的！不过…没关系，欢迎您随时再来！
t_en: Our prices are really, really affordable! But... no worries, please come back anytime!

t_ch: 年费有点贵，有没有其他选项？
t_en: The annual fee is a bit steep. Are there any other options?

t_ch: 不好意思，我们这里只提供会员服务。
t_en: Sorry, but our services are for members only.

t_ch: 不好意思，我对这个不感兴趣。
t_en: Sorry, I'm not interested.

t_ch: 没关系，欢迎您随时再来！
t_en: No worries, feel free to come back anytime!

t_ch: 欢迎光临卡拉OK的钢人，我们尊贵的终身会员{NICKNAME}{F#女士}{M#先生}！
t_en: Welcome to Karaoke Machine, our esteemed lifetime member {F#Madam}{M#Sir} {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 请问今天打算来欢唱一曲吗？
t_en: Are you here to sing your heart out today?

t_ch: 请帮我安排一间包厢吧！
t_en: Book me a private room, please.

t_ch: 我有些想要咨询一下的…
t_en: There's something I'd like to ask about...

t_ch: 当然了，希望了解什么呢？
t_en: Certainly! What would you like to know?

t_ch: 好像跟我在浴室哼的歌差不多…
t_en: Kinda sounds like what I hum in the shower...

t_ch: 是吗？那…我们的音响条件肯定是比您的浴室更好的！
t_en: Oh really? Well... I'm sure our sound system is way better than your bathroom!

t_ch: 我的包厢好像私密性不太好啊…
t_en: My room didn't feel all that private...

t_ch: 是吗？已经收到了您的反馈，我们将会仔细思考并努力改进。
t_en: Oh really? Your feedback has been received. We'll review it carefully and strive to improve.

t_ch: 唱完后，偶尔会感受到些什么…
t_en: Sometimes, after singing, a trace of something lingers in the air...

t_ch: 那是情绪价值！您得到了情绪价值！
t_en: That's emotional value! You earned emotional value!

t_ch: 我没什么想问的了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 不好意思，今天就算了。
t_en: Sorry, I'll pass for today.

t_ch: 没关系，您是尊贵的终身会员，我们随时欢迎您！
t_en: No problem at all. As our treasured lifetime member, you're always welcome here!

t_ch: 一曲唱罢，满心欢喜！如果现场还有其他的听众，想必他们也会感动得热泪盈眶——
t_en: What a performance! Such joy! If there were any other listeners here, they'd surely be moved to tears!

t_ch: 哎，究竟得是怎样顶级的音响器材，才能配得起你曾在浴室里无数次孤独的练习呢！
t_en: Ah, what kind of top-tier audio equipment could possibly do justice to all those lonely practice you've had in the bathroom, I wonder?

t_ch: 哎，要不是开拓的事业未竟，或许你应该找几个音乐同好组建乐队…当然了，如果你没有什么音乐梦想，也害怕与人交恶，那么…为了你好，还是少碰乐队吧。
t_en: Well, if it weren't for your grand journey of trailblazing, perhaps you'd have gathered a few fellow music lovers to form a band... Though, if you're not one for musical ambition, and not too fond of conflicts with others, then... for your own good, maybe it's best to steer clear of band life.

t_ch: 休息得差不多了，接下来你打算——
t_en: Now that you've rested enough, next you're planning to...

t_ch: 打电话，叫人来一起欢唱…
t_en: Call some friends, let's sing our hearts out...

t_ch: 独乐乐不如众乐乐！为什么不叫上你亲爱的小伙伴们一起来欢唱一番呢？
t_en: What joy is there in singing alone, when you can share it with friends? Let's invite your dear buddies to join in for a fun singing session.

t_ch: 平时很端着的杨叔也铆足了劲唱动画歌曲，姬子还点唱了几首只在二相乐园流行过的老歌。
t_en: Even the usually uptight Mr. Yang goes all out singing cartoon songs, while Himeko picks a few old tunes that have only been popular in Planarcadia.

t_ch: 丹恒又在假装高冷，一味地摇动沙锤，不让人知道他平时的小众宝藏歌单是什么……
t_en: Dan Heng is putting on his aloof act again, just shaking his rattle and refusing to let anyone know what niche treasures are hiding in his playlist...

t_ch: 星期日…他选了好几首他妹妹的大热歌曲，明明唱得挺好，总感觉他还是有点小腼腆。等等…难道说他只会这几首流行歌曲？
t_en: Sunday... He picks several of his sister's hit songs, and while he sings them pretty well, you can't help but notice he seems a bit shy. Wait... could it be that those are the only popular songs he knows?

t_ch: 小三月将话筒分给你，你们一起合唱了不少银河经典儿歌…哈哈，谁还不是个宝宝了？
t_en: March shares the mic with you, and you both sing a bunch of classic cosmic nursery rhymes together... Haha, who says you can't just be big babies at heart?

t_ch: …欢乐的时光总是显得很短暂，有机会的话，再一起去唱卡拉OK吧！
t_en: Ah... good times never seem to last long. Let's go do karaoke together again sometime!

t_ch: 保留此刻心情，点到为止。
t_en: Cherish this moment, then leave it at that.

t_ch: 嗯～你是个擅长满足的人，换句话说，你很擅长幸福。见好就收，好好体味当下的满足吧～
t_en: Mmm~ You're someone who's good at being content. In other words, you're skilled at finding happiness. Know when to be satisfied, and savor the contentment of the present moment~

t_ch: 有人或许说过，当一个人得到什么的时候，就忍不住想要更多…而「贪婪」总是在不同剧目中扮演着悲剧的起点，它似乎预兆着未来某个时刻，人会为追逐极端的幸福而坠落。
t_en: Someone once said that when we gain something, we immediately start wanting more. And often... that "greed" becomes the first act of every different tragedy. It seems to portend a future moment when one, in pursuit of extreme bliss, plummets into the abyss.

t_ch: 那么，能够及时满足，享乐当下是一项了不起的能力。
t_en: So, the ability to find immediate joy and fully embrace the present is a rare gift.

t_ch: 就像是捕鼠夹上的奶酪只是浅尝了一口，它的甜蜜还不足以撬动任何可怕的机关。
t_en: Like cheese on a mousetrap that's only been nibbled at, the sweetness alone wasn't enough to set off the deadly trap.

t_ch: 即便…现在还没满足也没有问题，那意味着：你始终保持着满心期待。
t_en: Even if... you're not satisfied right now, that's okay. It means you're always full of anticipation.

t_ch: 有空再来卡拉OK吧。
t_en: Come do karaoke again sometime.

t_ch: 接下来，我打算随意跑调！
t_en: Next up, I'm gonna sing off-key like there's no tomorrow!

t_ch: 要是在家里唱跑调了，未免有些不够有趣；要是在舞台上跑调了，未免有些解释不清。
t_en: Singing off-key at home isn't entertaining enough, and doing it onstage might need some explaining.

t_ch: 在卡拉OK的包厢里跑调？岂不是正好！你爱怎么唱就怎么唱，这是你的自由。
t_en: Singing off-key in a karaoke room? That's perfect! Sing however you want, it's your freedom.

t_ch: 一首歌应该怎么唱，法律并没有任何详细的规定。
t_en: There's no law that dictates exactly how a song should be sung.

t_ch: 或者，换句话说…你就是这间包厢里无上的法律、至尊的帝皇，你怎么唱，你的臣民们就该怎么唱。
t_en: Or in other words... you're the supreme law in this karaoke room, the almighty emperor. However you sing, your subjects must follow suit.

t_ch: 故意唱跑调了又如何呢？不唱屏幕上跳动的歌词又怎样呢？试着从「正确」绑架的人生中解放出来，这么一件小事不应该束缚你，千万别因为「正确」而停止了热情。
t_en: So what if you sing off-key? Who cares if you ignore the lyrics bouncing on the screen? Try breaking free from a life held hostage by "correctness." Never let something so trivial shackle you, and never let "correctness" snuff out your spark of passion.

t_ch: 要是有了兴致，你可以在这里呲哇乱叫！哇呜哇呜，崩恰啦咔咔，唔咻～咿哈！嘎啦玛！
t_en: Feel free to go wild and make some noise here! Woohoo, boom-chaka-laka, whoosh~ Yee-ha! Ga-lah-ma!

t_ch: 重要的是，当下的心情，它应该无拘无束！
t_en: What matters is your feeling in this very moment! Let it free!

t_ch: 也许大多数时候，我们要遵循「正确」，但下次你也可以回到这里，获得片刻的自由。
t_en: While we must often follow what's "correct," remember you can always return here for a moment of true freedom.

t_ch: 一曲唱罢，满心欢喜…嗯，怎么感觉包厢里的音量有点太大了。
t_en: What a performance! Such joy... Hmm, why does the karaoke room feel a bit too loud?

t_ch: 有点听不清自己说话了，只感觉颅骨嗡嗡地鸣响，那是喉咙通过骨传导找到的微弱存在感…你的耳朵呢？
t_en: You can barely hear your own voice, nothing but a low hum resonating in your skull, a faint presence your throat finds through bone conduction... What about your ears?

t_ch: 耳朵，只能听见那爆炸般的劲歌金曲！
t_en: All your ears get is a blast of those explosive, pounding hit songs!

t_ch: 这才是卡拉OK嘛，接下来你打算——
t_en: Now that's what they call karaoke! So next you're planning to...

t_ch: 音量调小，我要听见我的美声。
t_en: Turn down the volume, I need to hear my beautiful voice.

t_ch: 确实有点震耳欲聋了。你主动降低了一些伴奏的声响，但刚才的轰鸣还是影响了你的听感……
t_en: Indeed, it's a bit overwhelming. You lower the accompaniment volume, but that prior blast still leaves a dull ring in your ears...

t_ch: 似乎是为了补偿在音量上的亏损，你在下意识里更加声嘶力竭地唱所有的歌。
t_en: As if to compensate for the loss in volume, you subconsciously belt out every song even more forcefully.

t_ch: 伴奏就像是列阵的敌方军队，正在严阵以待。而你的歌声军队更是翻过了高山，顺着坡道，朝着伴奏方阵就是一个全军冲锋…两军相逢，气势更高者胜！
t_en: The accompaniment stands like enemy forces in formation, battle-ready. Meanwhile, your army of vocals has crossed the mountains, charging down the slope, launching a full-scale assault against the accompaniment's ranks... When two armies clash, victory belongs to the one with the mightier spirit!

t_ch: …明明只是唱歌而已，现在你才意识到自己已经充分调动了全身的肌肉！
t_en: And to think it's just singing... you only now realize it's put every muscle in your body to work!

t_ch: 是你，战胜了羸弱的伴奏！是你，更大声！
t_en: It is you who conquers the weak accompaniment! It is you who sings louder!

t_ch: 就连从隔音包厢外路过的服务员也不禁要感慨道「好大的音量！」
t_en: Even the waiters passing by the soundproof room can't help but exclaim "That's some crazy volume!"

t_ch: 下次回到卡拉OK的时候，带我们再赢一次吧！
t_en: Next time you're back at karaoke, let's crush it again!

t_ch: 可笑，伴奏还能有我唱得大声？
t_en: Ridiculous! How can the backup vocals be louder than my singing?

t_ch: 如果伴奏唱得有你本人大声，那些顶级的歌声是怎么回事，你明明听得一清二楚……
t_en: If the backing track is louder than you, then how come you can hear those top-tier vocals crystal clear...

t_ch: 或许声音也能通过量变完成质变，只要你唱得足够大声，那些成熟的技巧都会回归到你的心中。
t_en: Maybe a quantitative change in volume can lead to a qualitative leap. If you sing loud enough, all those refined techniques will naturally flow back into your heart.

t_ch: 渐入佳境后，你发现自己确实和过去不一样了，你随意的停顿就像是录音棚里反复录制的技巧。
t_en: As you get into the groove, you notice you are different from before. Your casual pauses are like practiced techniques from a recording studio.

t_ch: 你的真假声转换，你的高低音自如，就连音色…也能随着不同的歌曲随意变换。
t_en: Your ability to switch between chest and head voice, your control over high and low notes, and even your timbre... you can change it at will for different songs.

t_ch: 音乐之神，降临。
t_en: The God of Music has descended.

t_ch: 掌握全部音乐流派、技巧的满足感，在此刻达到最高潮！啊，原来，融汇贯通是这样的感觉啊。
t_en: The satisfaction of mastering all music genres and techniques culminates in this very moment! Ah, so this is true mastery.

t_ch: …后来，你在离开包厢时发现了两件事：卡拉OK歌曲全都打开了「原声」，并且你的话筒坏掉了。
t_en: ...Later, when you leave the karaoke room, you make two discoveries: all the songs have been set to "Original Voice," and your mic has given out the entire time.

t_ch: 这就是所谓的「无声自满」吧。
t_en: Guess this is what they call "Soundless Satisfaction."

t_ch: 一曲唱罢，满心欢喜…嗯？还能听见别的房间里传来的声音？！这包厢的隔音行不行啊！
t_en: What a performance! Such joy... Huh? You can still hear sounds from other rooms? The soundproofing in this karaoke room is trash!

t_ch: 也许，隔壁也会听见你的声音…毕竟，卡拉OK的命运是公平的。
t_en: Maybe your neighbors will hear you too... after all, karaoke karma is fair to everyone.

t_ch: …小小杂音，不过尔尔。接下来你打算——
t_en: ...Just a bit of static, no biggie. Next you are planning to...

t_ch: 忍耐，我早已经学会了忍耐。
t_en: Patience. I've long since learned the art of patience.

t_ch: 要是人类没有学会忍耐，那么人类终究不过是个烧水壶。
t_en: If humans never learned patience, they'd be nothing more than a kettle waiting to boil over.

t_ch: 忍耐，追求幸福，你要学会忍耐。
t_en: Be patient, pursue happiness, you must learn to endure.

t_ch: 可是那无休无止的土味音乐深深地拷打着你的音乐品味。
t_en: But that endless cringy music is torturing your musical taste to no end.

t_ch: 它们的「动次打次」以及戛然而止的切歌方式，正在以一种「入侵」的形态，干扰着你的忘情投入。
t_en: Their "boom-boom-pow" and abrupt song transitions are invading your space, disrupting your immersive experience.

t_ch: 但是没关系，你选择了忍耐！你的包容，是这座城市没有沦陷的唯一保险。
t_en: But it's okay, you choose to endure! Your tolerance is the only insurance keeping this city from falling.

t_ch: 如果全银河的人拥有你一半的耐心，那么银河将不再有战争！
t_en: If everyone in the cosmos had half your patience, there would be no more wars in the cosmos!

t_ch: 你的忍耐是你的最强的心之壁，你可以用它来对抗那些打扰清晨浅梦的装修电钻声！
t_en: Your patience is your mind's greatest fortress. Wield the power of it to fight against those construction drills that shatter your morning dreams!

t_ch: 忍不了，去找经理讨要说法！
t_en: You've had enough! Go give the manager a piece of your mind!

t_ch: 这种破包厢还敢收你年费会员，你必须找到他们的大堂经理讨要说法，至少换一间，对吧？
t_en: They have the nerve to charge you an annual membership fee for this dump of a room? You've got to find their manager and demand an explanation, at least get them to switch you to a different room, right?

t_ch: 你早知道的，卡拉OK的钢人是个热门的品牌，就算现在去询问，也不会有一间空闲的包厢。
t_en: Of course, it's no secret that the Karaoke Machine is always in high demand. There won't be a single room to be had, even if you ask now.

t_ch: 你早知道的，这些职业大堂经理最擅长的事，就是虽然还没有解决任何事，却好像付出了全部的努力一样。
t_en: You know their game. These professional lobby managers are the experts at making it look like they've moved heaven and earth without actually solving a single thing.

t_ch: 你早知道的，即便现在你去了新的包厢，但是你那愉快的心情却早就搁浅在旧的包厢之中了。
t_en: You know it deep down: even as you step into a new room, your joy remains stranded in the old one.

t_ch: 你早知道的，只要是出来玩，总会有可能被如此的*小概率事件*彻底摧毁。
t_en: You know it deep down: as long as you go out to have fun, it always carries a small risk of getting derailed by such bad luck.

t_ch: 至少，你不是什么都没有得到，你的心中还有「至死不渝的恨」。
t_en: At least you don't walk away empty-handed: you still have "Everlasting Hatred" within you.
